title: practical english usage - Part 321
content:
With object + adjective, the meaning is 'make somebody/something become’ .

As you get old, your memory gets worse.  My feet are getting cold.

Before an adjective, ger usually means 'become’ .

2 get    + adjective:      getting        old not Wayne's getting to be a lovely kid. (     Wayne's-gptting-a-lovely-kid;) we  can use get to be + noun (►   172.5 below).

Get + noun is not normally used to mean 'become'. To  express this meaning, I'll get you for this! (= punish, make suffer) I didn't get the joke. (= understand) Other meanings are sometimes possible.

Let me get you a drink.

Get can be used with two objects (►   8).

If you get a number 6 bus, it stops right outside our house.

If I listen to loud music I get a headache.

Can you come and get me from the station when I arrive?

I got an email from Lucy this morning.

‘ obtain’ , ‘ catch’  or something similar. The  exact meaning depends on the object.

With a direct object (noun or pronoun), get usually means 'receive', 'fetch', 1  get    + noun/pronoun:         I got    an   email.

to have'; with other kinds of word, the basic meaning is 'become', ‘ come to be'.

of word comes after it. With a direct object, the basic meaning is 'obtain', 'come in most kinds of speech and writing. The  meaning of get depends on what kind ways. It is sometimes avoided in a very formal style, but it is correct and natural Get is one of the commonest words in English, and is used in many different 472 get:   basic    st ructures      and   meanings      ______ ►    555 game      and   play ►    462 further       and   farther 'A funny thing happened? ‘ Do you mean funny ha-ha or funny peculiar?

Note that funny has another meaning: 'strange', 'peculiar'.

Why are you wearing that funny hat?

Funny  is an adjective, and is used to say that something makes you laugh.

Problems from A  to Z     Section Word                                    31 vocabulary' •  473   get and go: movement 394 ► get,    become,        go,   grow,     etc:  changes For get and go meaning 'become', ►    394.

Can you tell me how to get to the police station?

I don't know how we're going to get over the river.

It wasn 't easy to get through the crowd.

We   often use get to suggest that there is some difficulty in arriving.

I got to the meeting at about eight o'clock.

- 1 went to a meeting in Bristol yesterday.

1 usually get there first.

- I go to work by car and Lucy goes by train.

the arrival. Compare: Get is used when we  are thinking mainly about the end of a movement - Go is used to talk about a whole movement.

473 get    and   go:   move    ment    ___________ For causative and other uses of get + object + verb form, ►     108.

For get as a passive auxiliary (e.g. I get paid on Fridays), ►    60.

structure have got (►  24).

participle is gotten (e.g. You've gotten us in a lot of trouble) except in the In British English the past participle of get is got. In American English the past 6 got    and   gotten You'll get to speak English more easily as time goes by.

He's nice when you get to know him.

Get + infinitive can also suggest gradual development.

When do I get to meet your new boyfriend?

We didn't get to see her - she was too busy.

With an infinitive, get can mean 'manage', 'have an opportunity' or 'be allowed'.

We'd better get moving - it's late.

in the expressions get moving, get going.

Get.. .ing is sometimes used informally to mean 'start.. .ing', especially 5 get    .. .ing;   get    + infinitive You've got five minutes to get dressed.  She's getting married in June.

get washed, get dressed, get lost, get drowned, get engaged/married/divorced.

meaning, to talk about things that we  'do to ourselves'. Common expressions are Get can be used with a past participle. This structure often has a reflexive 4 get    + past    participle:      get    washed,        dressed,        married,         etc The car's OK  - it gets me from A to B.

Have you ever tried to get toothpaste back into the tube?

Would you mind getting your papers off my desk?

You can't get him  out of bed in the morning.
